definition: A claim whose stated relation can be represented by deterministic code or a finite numerical assertion inside this repository.
evidence_required: Validation-matrix row, formula-registry row where applicable, implementation path, named pytest assertion, and passing coverage gate.
repo_action: Keep in data/validation_matrix.csv and data/formula_registry.csv with status internally_validated.
response_rule: Answer with the exact paper claim, implementation, test name, and the boundary that this proves internal consistency only.

definition: A broad physical or theoretical claim represented by a deliberately limited computational proxy.
evidence_required: Proxy equation, boundedness or conservation test, explicit external-validation note, and no wording that treats the proxy as empirical proof.
repo_action: Keep as bounded_proxy and state what derivation or measurement would be needed to upgrade it.
response_rule: Answer by naming the proxy and the missing external work; do not overclaim.

definition: A claim that is conceptually central but still lacks a complete mathematical derivation or accepted mapping to physical quantities.
evidence_required: A precise missing derivation target, unresolved assumptions, and the conditions under which the claim would become executable or falsifiable.
repo_action: Keep in research-boundary text or open issues until formalized; do not count as validated theory.
response_rule: Accept the critique as useful pressure and translate it into a derivation task.

definition: A claim whose truth depends on independent measurement, observation, or accepted external datasets.
evidence_required: Named external dataset, measurement channel, uncertainty budget, and falsification threshold.
repo_action: Track in falsification criteria or external-validation notes; do not mark as empirically proven from internal tests.
response_rule: Answer by separating repository consistency from external confirmation.

definition: A source, reception, symbolic, cultural, or interpretive claim that cannot itself be validated by Python tests.
evidence_required: A clear statement that the claim is contextual rather than an executable theorem; if relevant, a proposed observation or archival record outside the validation matrix.
repo_action: Keep outside the evidence chain unless it is converted into a formal claim with a testable definition.
response_rule: Do not present as proof. Treat comments about framing, clarity, or reception as communication data.

definition: A critique that the repo presentation creates avoidable distrust, pathology framing, or category confusion before the technical argument can be reviewed.
evidence_required: Concrete wording, file-history, README, title, or outreach artifact that creates the risk.
repo_action: Neutralize unnecessary framing, document the change, and keep public materials paper/code/validation first.
response_rule: Acknowledge the perception risk, point to the cleanup, and redirect to paper/code/test substance.
